The system is an airport runway scheduling system.
The system coordinates aircraft arrivals and departures.
The system operates across two runways.
The system registers incoming flights with their estimated times.
The system registers outgoing flights with their estimated times.
The system allocates a time slot to each flight.
Each time slot is 5 minutes long.
The system allocates the earliest available slot to each flight.
The system allocates slots on either runway.
The allocated slot is at or after the flight's estimated time.
There is a 3-minute separation gap between consecutive slots on the same runway.
Arrivals have priority over departures when competing for the same slot.
The system detects overlapping assignments on the same runway.
The system prevents overlapping assignments on the same runway.
The system detects gap-violating assignments on the same runway.
The system prevents gap-violating assignments on the same runway.
The system allows a runway to be closed.
When a runway is closed, the system reassigns its future flights to the other runway.
When a runway is closed, the system marks flights as delayed if they cannot be placed within 60 minutes of their estimated time.
The system immediately gives a declared emergency flight the earliest possible slot.
The system re-queues displaced flights by the normal rules.
The system shows a slot timetable per runway.
The slot timetable includes delayed flights.
The slot timetable includes emergency events.
It is a platform that manages the sequencing of plane landings and takeoffs on a pair of airstrips.
This system assigns departure and arrival slots while enforcing a minimum separation interval between consecutive uses of the same runway.
Controllers can register flight plans and the system automatically finds the earliest feasible slot.
When an emergency is declared, the system instantly frees up a slot and reorders other flights to accommodate it.
An airport slot coordinator that handles time allocation for inbound and outbound aircraft across two active runways, with built-in conflict detection and emergency override.
